title: OpenAI's new release turns a bad week ugly for software stocks
excerpt: OpenAI's Presence tool challenges software vendors, contributing to a drop in stock prices on the IGV index.
content_html:
By Stephen Council

You're currently following this author! Want to unfollow? Unsubscribe via the link in your email.

OpenAI just handed software stocks a beating with its latest push into enterprise work.

After the company revealed a new product on Wednesday meant to make AI agents more effective for customers, software stocks like Workday, Atlassian, and HubSpot tumbled.

The product, called Presence, ramps up OpenAI's competition with established software vendors. As Business Insider first reported, the tool lets companies create guardrails, permissions, and policies around how AI agents use company data. It's meant to automate work in customer support, sales, internal processes, and other areas.

The release is part of OpenAI's broader strategy to build software capabilities and governance on top of its AI models. It pushes OpenAI further into the SaaS, or software-as-a-service, market, where established companies have been on edge about the impacts of AI coding tools and AI agents.

Stocks in the sector fell on Wednesday and Thursday — already a tough stretch amid an oil price surge, rising bond yields, and worrying Big Tech earnings.

Since Wednesday morning, share prices have dropped for Workday (-9.9%), Atlassian (-11.8%), HubSpot (-12.7%), Salesforce (-7.7%), and Okta (-4.2%).

In a note to investors, TD Cowen analysts wrote that the Presence announcement was likely a "major reason" for a 3% sell-off in the IGV software index on Wednesday and further declines on Thursday.

"Presence is being marketed with much of the AI Agent capabilities that SaaS vendors pitch, including packaging LLM reasoning + data access + governance/policy control," the analysts wrote.

Derrick Wood, a tech analyst from the TD Cowen team, told Business Insider that among the business applications companies that overlap with Presence, he sees the customer service and sales fields as particularly exposed.

Wood pointed back to early October for precedent, when a series of blog posts from OpenAI showcased internal AI tools. The tools resembled SaaS offerings, and OpenAI's posts sent software stocks like HubSpot, DocuSign, and ZoomInfo reeling.

At the time, TD Cowen analysts wrote that the reaction was overblown, but noted, "days like this remind investors of how much concern there is in the market around 'AI eating software'."

OpenAI did not respond to a request for comment.

content_html_zh:
作者：Stephen Council 您目前正在关注该作者！ 想要取消关注吗？ 通过电子邮件中的链接取消订阅。

OpenAI 最近进军企业工作，给软件股带来了打击。

在该公司周三发布了一款旨在使人工智能代理更有效地为客户服务的新产品后，Workday、Atlassian 和 HubSpot 等软件股票暴跌。

该产品名为 Presence，增强了 OpenAI 与成熟软件供应商的竞争。 正如 Business Insider 首先报道的那样，该工具可以让公司围绕人工智能代理如何使用公司数据创建护栏、权限和策略。 它旨在实现客户支持、销售、内部流程和其他领域的工作自动化。

此次发布是 OpenAI 在其人工智能模型之上构建软件功能和治理的更广泛战略的一部分。 它将 OpenAI 进一步推向 SaaS（软件即服务）市场，该市场的老牌公司一直对 AI 编码工具和 AI 代理的影响感到紧张。

该行业的股票在周三和周四下跌——在油价飙升、债券收益率上升以及大型科技公司盈利令人担忧的情况下，该行业的股价已经下跌。

自周三上午以来，Workday (-9.9%)、Atlassian (-11.8%)、HubSpot (-12.7%)、Salesforce (-7.7%) 和 Okta (-4.2%) 的股价均下跌。

TD Cowen 分析师在给投资者的一份报告中写道，Presence 的公告可能是 IGV 软件指数周三下跌 3% 以及周四进一步下跌的“主要原因”。

分析师写道：“Presence 正在与 SaaS 供应商宣传的许多 AI Agent 功能一起进行营销，包括打包 LLM 推理+数据访问+治理/策略控制。”

TD Cowen 团队的技术分析师 Derrick Wood 告诉 Business Insider，在与 Presence 重叠的商业应用公司中，他认为客户服务和销售领域尤其暴露。

Wood 回顾了 10 月初的先例，当时 OpenAI 的一系列博客文章展示了内部人工智能工具。 这些工具类似于 SaaS 产品，OpenAI 的帖子让 HubSpot、DocuSign 和 ZoomInfo 等软件股票大幅下跌。

当时，TD Cowen 分析师写道，这种反应有些言过其实，但他指出，“这样的日子提醒投资者，市场对‘人工智能饮食软件’有多么担忧。”

OpenAI 没有回应置评请求。
